title: Multi-stressor experiments with Dendrophyllia cornigera: activities conducted and next steps
author: Cristina Gutiérrez-Zárate
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This presentation will give an update about the ongoing work to study the ecophysiological response of the Cold-Water Coral  Dendrophyllia cornigera  to multiple anthropogenic stressors under global change scenarios (WP4.3). We have designed and set up an aquaria facility that allows for the control and manipulation of several water parameters (namely temperature, carbon dioxide and oxygen concentrations) in order to study their combined effects and possible interactions on the ecophysiological response of  D. cornigera . The experimental design includes two scenarios: current in situ natural temperature, carbon dioxide and oxygen concentration values vs IPCC RCP 8.5 “business as usual” prediction, and allows for the performance of medium to long-term ecophysiological experiments. The results from the performed experiments will be an important contribution to better understand the vulnerability of deep benthic communities to global change.
